channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Musk doubles down on email ultimatum; Patel tells FBI staff to wait
item.description: Newly-appointed FBI Director Kash Patel sent a message to the FBI workforce on Saturday night telling them to not reply to the emails.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth defends Trump's firings of Pentagon leaders and says there may be more dismissals
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is defending President Donald Trump’s firing of the nation’s senior military officer and a wave of top dismissals at the Pentagon, insisting that they weren’t unusual despite accusations that the new administration is injecting politics into the military.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Veterans ask Trump to award Medal of Honor to Marine Corps war hero snubbed by Biden
item.description: A group of veterans and lawmakers is urging President Donald Trump to award an 87-year-old Marine Corps "legend" and war hero the Medal of Honor after he was snubbed by former President Joe Biden.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New FBI Director Kash Patel will also be named acting head of the ATF, official says
item.description: New FBI Director Kash Patel is expected to be named the acting head of the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Coal still key to US energy dominance, says West Virginia governor
item.description: West Virginia Republican Gov. Patrick Morrisey touted the Mountain State's coal infrastructure as the unsung key to battling China in energy dominance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former British PM reveals which Trump department is perfect 'playbook' for conservative revolution
item.description: Former Prime Minister of the United Kingdom Liz Truss, the shortest-serving prime minister in British history, said world leaders are envious of Trump’s second term and DOGE.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge largely blocks Trump‘s executive orders ending federal support for DEI programs
item.description: A U.S. district judge granted a preliminary injunction blocking the administration from terminating or changing federal contracts they consider equity-related.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Meet four Minnesota federal workers mourning the loss of their dream jobs
item.description: Many Minnesotans are among those facing unemployment, as the Trump administration slashes jobs across agencies. The state is home to more than 18,000 federal workers, with nearly 60 percent working for the Department of Veterans Affairs and the U.S. Department of Agriculture.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Trump moves with light speed and brute force in shaking the core of what America has been
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is moving with light speed and brute force to break the existing order and reshape America at home and abroad. He likes the ring of calling himself king. No one can absorb it all.
item.dc:creator: CALVIN WOODWARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NIH funding freeze stalls applications on $1.5 billion in medical research funds
item.description: The National Institutes of Health had to stop considering new grant applications, delaying funding for research into diseases ranging from heart disease and cancer to Alzheimer’s and allergies.
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein, MPR News

item.title: DeSantis reappoints Moms for Liberty cofounder to Florida ethics commission
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis is attempting to put Moms for Liberty cofounder Tina Descovich on the Florida Commission on Ethics for the second time.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: After mass layoffs, some federal agencies are trying to bring employees back
item.description: A number of federal agencies, from HHS to the USDA, have laid off employees only to rescind those terminations days later. An expert says it "suggests something about mismanagement of government.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: Politics Friday: Crime, public safety measures drive debates at the Capitol
item.description: MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst and his guests talk about new public safety bills and criminal justice debates at the Legislature.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: 5 takeaways from Elon Musk's CPAC talk
item.description: Elon Musk served as one of the main attractions at the Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) with a late-scheduled appearance that saw him wield a chainsaw.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Senate Republicans approve budget framework, pushing past Democratic objections after all-night vote
item.description: The early Friday vote was a key step toward providing money the Trump administration says it needs funds for mass deportations and security measures that top the Republican agenda.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Justice Department broadens Jan. 6 pardons to cover gun, drug-related charges
item.description: In a new development, the Department of Justice said Trump's pardon of Jan. 6 defendants should apply more broadly and include separate gun charges, as well.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dreisbach, MPR News

item.title: Planned medical research grant cuts worry Sarasota scientists
item.description: For more than 20 years, Sarasota-based biomedical research facility The Roskamp Institute has been focused on understanding and finding treatment, prevention, and early detection for Alzheimer's disease and other dementia, along with illnesses facing veterans.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge rules Trump administration can move ahead on mass government layoffs
item.description: A federal judge Thursday refused a group of government employee unions’ request to block the Trump administration from moving ahead with plans to dramatically reduce the federal workforce. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Florida sues Target for 'defrauding investors' by selling LGBTQ+ Pride merchandise
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier on Thursday announced the state filed a class action lawsuit against Target claiming "the company knowingly misled and defrauded investors by concealing the financial risks of its radical LGBTQ activism.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Speaker Johnson discourages DOGE stimulus checks idea
item.description: That marks a break from President Trump and tech billionaire and Trump adviser Elon Musk, who have floated the idea in recent days.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: First CDC vaccine meeting under Trump administration is postponed
item.description: The panel of vaccine experts were supposed to hold their first meeting under the Trump administration in late February. It’s not clear when that meeting will now take place.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone, MPR News

item.title: Ellison: Trump order on transgender athletes violates Minnesota law
item.description: Attorney General Keith Ellison’s opinion comes days after the Trump administration said it would investigate the state high school league’s plan to follow state law and let students participate in sports consistent with their gender identity, despite the president’s executive order.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Shockman and Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: USDA to follow ‘president’s lead’ on foreign farm ownership policy
item.description: The concern, however, is that international ownership could lead to risks of spying or economic pressure.
item.dc:creator: Alex Caprariello, NewsNation

item.title: Former NFL player Chris Kluwe arrested while protesting MAGA sign at city council meeting
item.description: Former NFL punter Chris Kluwe was detained at a city council meeting in California on Tuesday after delivering an impassioned speech criticizing the design of a plaque to be installed at the Huntington Beach Central Library.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Frey vetoes council move toward a pedestrian plaza at George Floyd Square
item.description: Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey has vetoed the City Council’s plan to explore building a pedestrian-only mall at George Floyd Square, the intersection where an officer killed Floyd in 2020.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Trump may buy used planes to serve as Air Force One amid Boeing delays
item.description: It's not the planes, rather the heavy modification to make them suitable for the requirements of presidential travel and the top-level security clearances required for those involved, that has added to the cost and delays.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A military vet and a scientist were securing America’s food system. Trump fired them
item.description: More than 10,000 federal employees who had yet to complete their probationary periods have been fired by the Trump administration, including those who work to protect American agriculture.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Black farmers among those left in limbo amid federal funding freeze
item.description: The federal government has historically discriminated against Black farmers. The recent funding halt from the Trump administration presents yet another hurdle to those who have spent decades fighting for equity in farming.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Peñaloza, MPR News

item.title: Gov. Hochul, MTA vow to fight President Trump over congestion pricing
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The MTA is suing the Trump administration after it announced plans to scrap New York City’s congestion pricing toll on Wednesday. A letter sent from U.S. Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy to Gov.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Latino business owners say customers are staying away in wake of deportation threats
item.description: Some Latino business owners say customers have been staying away out of fear of being detained and deported. Twin Cities business and city leaders are encouraging people to buy from Latino-owned shops this Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Regina Medina, MPR News

item.title: Fentanyl on coroners' radar long before Nevada lawmakers took notice
item.description: The coroner's office knows what's happening in the community long before lawmakers do. That became clear during a discussion of deaths on Nevada's roads in Carson City on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump signals 25 percent tariffs coming on cars, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday indicated he would unveil tariffs on imports of automobiles, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals in early April, starting at 25 percent.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC
